question: The graph represents the motion of a bus travelling along a straight, level road between two stops. [Diagram: v-t graph showing acceleration from 0 to 9 m/s over 20s, constant velocity of 9 m/s for 40s, and deceleration to 0 over 20s.] [cite: 21]

parts.text: Describe the motion of the bus between A and B.
Describe the motion of the bus between B and C.
Calculate the acceleration of the bus between C and D.
Determine the distance travelled by the bus between A and D.

question: The triathlon is an endurance race consisting of three stages: swimming, cycling, and running. [cite: 22]

parts.text: Determine the magnitude of the resultant of these forces (25 N at 090 and 12 N at 180).
Determine the direction of the resultant of these forces.
The triathlete has a mass of 75 kg. Calculate the acceleration of the triathlete.
Suggest one way in which the triathlete could reduce the frictional forces acting against them when cycling.
parts.answer: Streamlined position / streamlined helmet / thinner tyres / lubricate chain.
parts.markingScheme: • Any valid suggestion related to reducing friction (1) [cite: 28]

parts.text: The triathlete takes 38 minutes to complete four laps of a 2.5 km course. Determine the average speed.
State the magnitude of the average velocity of the triathlete for this stage.

question: A skateboarder and board have a combined mass of 65 kg. They slide along a 2.0 m horizontal rail. [cite: 32, 33]

parts.text: Determine the decrease in kinetic energy as the speed changes from 7.0 m/s to 3.0 m/s.
Calculate the average frictional force between the rail and the skateboard.
Sketch the path of the skateboarder after leaving the rail. [Diagram: horizontal rail above ground.]
parts.markingScheme: • Curved path from end of rail to ground (1) [cite: 40]

question: NASA is planning a crewed mission to the Moon. [cite: 41]

parts.text: Calculate the weight of a 67 kg astronaut at an altitude of 140 km above the Moon (where g = 1.4 N/kg from graph).
Describe one physics-related challenge these astronauts will face while on the surface of the Moon.
parts.answer: Lack of atmosphere / extreme temperatures / cosmic radiation / low gravity effects on body.

parts.text: State and justify what will happen to the acceleration of the transportation module if rockets exert a constant upward force.
parts.markingScheme:
• Acceleration increases (1)
• Mass decreases as fuel is used (1)
• $a = F/m$ so as $m$ decreases, $a$ increases (1) [cite: 51]

question: Using your knowledge of physics, comment on Copernicus' model of the Universe (Sun motionless at centre, stars fixed). [cite: 52]

parts.text: Using your knowledge of physics, comment on this model.
parts.markingScheme: Marks based on breadth and depth of physics knowledge (e.g., Sun orbits Galaxy, stars are not fixed, expansion of universe). (3) [cite: 741]

question: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) is a space-based science observatory. [cite: 53]

parts.text: State an advantage of using a space-based telescope compared to ground-based telescopes.
parts.answer: No atmospheric interference / can detect wavelengths absorbed by atmosphere.

parts.text: State what is meant by the term exoplanet.
parts.answer: A planet outside of our solar system (orbiting another star).
parts.markingScheme: • Planet orbiting a star other than the Sun (1) [cite: 57]

parts.text: Determine the distance in metres to a star 41 light-years from Earth.
Determine which elements (Hydrogen, Helium, Mercury, Nitrogen) are present in the star based on the line spectra.

question: A wireless charger uses radio waves to charge the battery of a mobile phone. [cite: 61]

parts.text: Explain in terms of electron flow what is meant by direct current.
parts.answer: Electrons flow in only one direction.
parts.markingScheme: • Flow of electrons in one direction only (1) [cite: 63]

parts.text: Calculate the charge supplied in 1.5 hours if the current is 2.5 A.
Explain how a thermistor-based circuit operates to switch on a fan as temperature increases.
parts.markingScheme:
• Temperature increases, thermistor resistance decreases (1)
• Voltage across thermistor decreases / voltage across variable resistor increases (1)
• Transistor switches on, activating fan (1) [cite: 69]

parts.text: An LED has 2.2 V and 18 mA. Determine the resistance of the series resistor connected to a 5.0 V supply.

question: A student investigates the relationship between current and voltage for a lamp. [cite: 73]

parts.text: Draw a graph of the results (Current vs Voltage table provided).
parts.markingScheme: • Axes labeled and scaled (1), points plotted (1), curve of best fit (1) [cite: 76]

parts.text: Determine the voltage when current is 0.70 A using the graph.
Describe how the student obtained a range of values using the circuit.
Sketch a V-I graph for a fixed resistor.

question: A microwave oven is used to heat 0.020 kg of water to sterilise a baby bottle. [cite: 80, 82]

parts.text: Calculate the energy required to heat 0.020 kg water from 6.3 °C to 100 °C.
Calculate the energy to change 0.014 kg of water at 100 °C to steam.
Determine the minimum energy to produce 0.014 kg of steam from the initial state.
Calculate total energy used by a 750 W microwave in 180 s.
Explain why total energy used is different from the minimum energy required.
parts.answer: Energy is lost to the surroundings / heating the bottle.

parts.text: Determine the pressure at 14 °C if initial pressure is 655 kPa at 21 °C (volume constant).
Explain using the kinetic model how decrease in temperature affects pressure.
parts.markingScheme:
• Speed/kinetic energy of particles decreases (1)
• Particles hit walls less frequently / with less force (1)
• Total force on walls decreases (1) [cite: 97]

parts.text: Tyres have area $7.5 \times 10^{-4}\text{ m}^2$ and pressure $1.02 \times 10^6\text{ Pa}$. Determine the total mass.

question: A wave energy converter anchored to the seabed. [cite: 100]

parts.text: State what is meant by the term transverse wave.
parts.answer: Oscillations are at right angles to the direction of energy transfer.
parts.markingScheme: • Vibration at right angles to energy direction (1) [cite: 101]

parts.text: Calculate frequency if one wave takes 7.4 s to pass.
Suggest how accuracy of frequency determination could be improved.
parts.answer: Measure time for multiple waves and divide.

question: Water waves approaching a gap in a harbour wall. [Diagram: Waves entering a gap.] [cite: 109]

parts.text: State the name given to the effect shown in the diagram.
The gap is 20 m. Long wavelength waves (35 m) are now used. State the effect on the waves after passing through the gap.

question: A scanner uses X-rays to detect hidden objects. [cite: 112]

parts.text: State one other application of X-rays in medicine.
Calculate the wavelength of X-rays with a frequency of $2.5 \times 10^{17}\text{ Hz}$.
Comment on the statement about electrons 'whizzing round' and turning into light.
parts.markingScheme: Open-ended; credit for identifying misconceptions (energy conversion vs electron conversion). (3) [cite: 741]

question: A worker in a nuclear power station is exposed to radiation. [cite: 119]

parts.text: Calculate the equivalent dose for an absorbed dose of 0.50 mGy from alpha (Wr=20).
The worker has a mass of 80 kg. Calculate the energy absorbed.

question: Nuclear reactors use fission to generate electricity. [cite: 122]

parts.text: State what is meant by the term nuclear fission.
parts.answer: A large nucleus splitting into smaller nuclei, releasing energy and neutrons.

parts.text: Describe the role of neutrons in a chain reaction.
parts.answer: Neutrons released in one fission hit other nuclei, causing more fission.
parts.markingScheme: • Neutrons cause further fission (1) • Chain reaction continues (1) [cite: 124]
